last_digest.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
last_digest.rss_summary: The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.

last_digest.title: CISA orders feds to patch Windows flaw exploited as zero-day
last_digest.rss_summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to secure their Windows systems against a vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

last_digest.title: Microsoft's patch for a 0-day exploited by Russian spies fell short. Another Windows flaw is under attack
last_digest.rss_summary: Second try's a charm? Microsoft and the US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned that attackers are exploiting a zero-click Windows flaw that can expose sensitive information on vulnerable systems.…

last_digest.title: Large-scale Roblox hacking operation shut down by Ukrainian authorities
last_digest.rss_summary: Ukrainian police arrested three hackers who hijacked 610,000 Roblox accounts and sold them for $225,000 in profit. Police in Ukraine arrested three suspects accused of hacking over 610,000 Roblox accounts and selling them for about $225,000. Officers carried out multiple searches in Lviv, seizing cash, phones, computers, laptops, tablets, and USB drives. The operation disrupted […]

last_digest.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
last_digest.rss_summary:
The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code.
The post Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_week_digest.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
last_week_digest.tldr: France Titres, the agency managing national identity documents, confirmed a breach involving the ants.gouv.fr portal. While the agency investigates, a threat actor claims to have stolen 19 million records, including names and contact details. This incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of state infrastructure to identity-focused cyberattacks. It signals a growing industry trend where government databases become prime targets for mass data extortion, necessitating heightened vigilance against sophisticated phishing.

last_week_digest.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
last_week_digest.tldr: British national Tyler Buchanan has pleaded guilty to wire fraud and identity theft as a senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group. His activities, involving tens of thousands of phishing attacks, compromised at least twelve major technology firms and siphoned over $8 million in cryptocurrency. This conviction highlights the escalating operational threat posed by social engineering syndicates, signalling a shift toward more robust international law enforcement cooperation.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware negotiator caught secretly assisting BlackCat extortion scheme
last_week_digest.tldr: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the BlackCat cybercriminal group, having secretly leaked sensitive client data across five cases to inflate extortion demands. This insider betrayal, which involved extorting $1.2 million from a single victim, highlights a critical vulnerability in incident response trust. The case signals an urgent industry need for stricter vetting and oversight of cybersecurity professionals who hold privileged access to victim data.

last_week_digest.title: $290 Million Kelp DAO Crypto Heist Blamed on North Korea
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-sponsored actors successfully executed a sophisticated $290 million heist targeting Kelp DAO by compromising LayerZero’s distributed validator network infrastructure. This breach, which involved manipulating remote procedure calls to force failovers into poisoned systems, highlights the severe operational risks inherent in decentralised finance protocols. The incident signals a growing industry trend where attackers exploit complex cross-chain dependencies, forcing organisations to prioritise infrastructure resilience against persistent nation-state threats.

last_week_digest.title: Yet another ex-ransomware negotiator admits turning rogue after payoff from crimelords
last_week_digest.tldr: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to colluding with the ALPHV/BlackCat gang, betraying five client firms by leaking confidential insurance data to inflate ransom demands. These victims, including a nonprofit that paid $26.8 million, suffered significant financial losses due to this insider threat. This case signals a critical erosion of trust in incident response services, forcing global organisations to implement stricter vetting and oversight of third-party security partners.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean hackers siphon more than $12 million from crypto users in sprawling campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-backed hackers, identified as HexagonalRodent, siphoned over $12 million from 26,584 cryptocurrency wallets across 2,726 infected systems in early 2026. By leveraging generative AI to orchestrate sophisticated job-recruitment scams targeting software developers, these actors bypassed traditional security protocols. This campaign signals a strategic shift toward high-volume, opportunistic attacks on individuals, exploiting global labour market vulnerabilities to diversify revenue streams beyond traditional, large-scale cryptocurrency exchange heists.

last_week_digest.title: Cyberattack on French government agency triggers phishing alert
last_week_digest.tldr: France Titres, the agency managing national identity documents, recently suffered a significant data breach exposing personal information including names, birth dates, and contact details for an undisclosed number of citizens. This incident forces the organisation to implement heightened security measures and warns users against targeted phishing campaigns. The breach highlights the persistent vulnerability of critical government infrastructure, signalling an urgent need for robust identity protection across Europe.

last_week_digest.title: GopherWhisper APT group hides command and control traffic in Slack and Discord
last_week_digest.tldr: The China-aligned GopherWhisper group has compromised a Mongolian government entity by leveraging legitimate platforms like Slack, Discord, and Outlook for command and control traffic. By embedding malicious operations within standard enterprise communication channels, the attackers successfully exfiltrated data while masking their activity. This trend signals a critical shift in threat actor tactics, forcing global organisations to reconsider how they monitor trusted collaboration tools for hidden malicious communication.

last_week_digest.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
last_week_digest.tldr: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting a high-severity command-injection vulnerability in end-of-life D-Link DIR-823X routers. Since the vendor will not issue patches for these discontinued devices, businesses face significant risks from botnet-driven DDoS attacks. This incident signals a growing industry trend where attackers specifically target legacy hardware, highlighting the urgent operational necessity for organisations to decommission unsupported infrastructure to maintain robust global network security.

last_week_digest.title: Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says
last_week_digest.tldr: The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre warns that state-sponsored actors from Russia, Iran, and China now pose the most significant threats to British businesses. Should international geopolitical tensions escalate, domestic firms face the risk of large-scale cyberattacks targeting critical operations. This shift signals a new era where commercial entities must treat national security as a core business priority, as digital infrastructure becomes a primary battlefield during global conflicts.

last_week_digest.title: With AI’s help, North Korean hackers stumbled into a near-undetectable attack
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean threat group HexagonalRodent is leveraging generative AI tools like Cursor and ChatGPT to orchestrate sophisticated social engineering campaigns against Web3 developers. By automating malware development and persona management, these actors successfully infiltrate soft targets holding significant digital assets. This shift signals a dangerous industry trend where AI lowers the barrier to entry for state-sponsored operations, enabling even less skilled attackers to execute highly convincing, near-undetectable cyberattacks.

last_week_digest.title: China-linked threat actors use consumer device botnets to evade detection, warn UK and partners
last_week_digest.tldr: China-linked threat actors are increasingly leveraging vast botnets of hijacked consumer devices, such as routers and cameras, to mask malicious traffic and evade detection. This shift renders static IP blocklists largely ineffective, forcing organisations to confront the rapid extinction of indicators of compromise. For global businesses, this signals a critical need to move beyond traditional perimeter defences toward adaptive, intelligence-driven strategies, including zero-trust controls and anomaly detection.

last_week_digest.title: Luxury cosmetics giant Rituals discloses data breach impacting member personal details
last_week_digest.tldr: Luxury cosmetics firm Rituals has confirmed a data breach involving the unauthorised download of personal member information, including names, addresses, and birth dates. While payment details remained secure, the incident exposes the growing vulnerability of premium lifestyle brands to targeted database exfiltration. This breach highlights the persistent risk to consumer trust and data privacy, signalling that even high-revenue global retailers must urgently bolster defences against sophisticated unauthorised access attempts.

last_week_digest.title: CISA: US agency breached through Cisco vulnerability, FIRESTARTER backdoor allowed access through March
last_week_digest.tldr: Sophisticated threat actors breached a federal agency using Cisco firewall vulnerabilities, deploying FIRESTARTER malware to maintain persistent access through March. By bypassing VPN authentication, attackers compromised administrative credentials and private keys, demonstrating significant operational risk. This incident highlights a critical industry shift where state-sponsored groups focus on long-term persistence within core infrastructure, forcing global organisations to reassess the security of consolidated network appliances against advanced, stealthy persistent threats.

last_week_digest.title: Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: The malicious compromise of a Bitwarden NPM package by the TeamPCP group, linked to the Shai-Hulud worm, highlights a critical vulnerability within global software supply chains. This incident threatens the integrity of downstream applications, potentially exposing sensitive user credentials and operational data. It signals an urgent industry-wide requirement for enhanced dependency auditing and rigorous provenance verification to mitigate the escalating risk of automated, large-scale malicious code injection attacks.

last_week_digest.title: ADT confirms data breach after ShinyHunters leak threat
last_week_digest.tldr: Home security provider ADT confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group claimed to have stolen 10 million records via a vishing attack on an employee’s SSO account. While ADT maintains that sensitive payment details remain secure, the incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of SaaS platforms to credential theft. This breach signals an industry-wide shift where attackers increasingly exploit corporate identity providers to exfiltrate vast datasets for extortion.

last_week_digest.title: GopherWhisper: new China-linked APT targets Mongolia with Go-based malware
last_week_digest.tldr: The China-aligned threat actor GopherWhisper has targeted Mongolian government institutions, compromising at least 12 systems through a sophisticated suite of Go-based malware. By abusing legitimate platforms like Slack and Outlook for command-and-control, the group maintained persistent access to sensitive state infrastructure. This campaign highlights a growing industry trend where state-sponsored actors leverage common cloud services to mask espionage, forcing organisations to rethink their third-party application security strategies.

last_week_digest.title: Week in review: Claude Mythos finds 271 Firefox flaws, Vercel breach
last_week_digest.tldr: Recent cybersecurity developments highlight escalating risks, with cyber insurance claims reaching record highs as ransomware severity averages $508,000. Simultaneously, the proliferation of sophisticated tools like the ATHR voice-phishing platform and NGate Android malware demonstrates how automation lowers barriers for global threat actors. These trends signal that businesses must shift from reactive security postures toward proactive, AI-driven red-teaming and robust infrastructure monitoring to mitigate the rising costs of digital operations.

last_week_digest.title: Iran’s cyber threat may be less ‘shock and awe’ than ‘low and slow,’ officials say
last_week_digest.tldr: Iranian cyber operations are shifting toward opportunistic, low-and-slow intrusions rather than large-scale disruptive campaigns. By exploiting basic security gaps and stolen credentials, attackers recently disabled thousands of medical devices at Stryker. This trend signals that nation-state threats increasingly mirror criminal tactics, prioritising identity theft and social engineering over sophisticated malware. For global organisations, this confirms that securing basic access controls remains the most critical defence against state-sponsored actors.

last_week_digest.title: US Federal Agency’s Cisco Firewall Infected With ‘Firestarter’ Backdoor
last_week_digest.tldr: A federal agency’s Cisco firewall was compromised by the Firestarter backdoor, enabling persistent remote access despite subsequent security patching. This breach highlights critical vulnerabilities in perimeter defence infrastructure, demonstrating how sophisticated actors maintain long-term footholds within government networks. Such incidents signal a growing industry-wide challenge where attackers bypass traditional mitigation strategies, forcing organisations to rethink their reliance on single-vendor hardware security and prioritise advanced, continuous threat hunting protocols.

last_week_digest.title: Medtronic discloses security incident after ShinyHunters claimed theft of 9M+ records
last_week_digest.tldr: Medical device giant Medtronic confirmed a significant security breach after the threat actor ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen over 9 million records from its corporate IT systems. While the company maintains that patient safety and manufacturing operations remain unaffected, the incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of global healthcare infrastructure to large-scale data exfiltration, signalling an urgent need for organisations to further isolate critical networks from broader corporate environments.

last_week_digest.title: North Korean Hackers Target Crypto Firms with ClickFix and AI-Made Zoom Lures
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean hacking group BlueNoroff has compromised over 100 cryptocurrency organisations across 20 countries using sophisticated AI-driven social engineering. By deploying fake Zoom interfaces and clipboard injection attacks, the threat actors maintained system access for 66 days, specifically targeting executive credentials and digital wallets. This campaign highlights a dangerous shift toward automated deepfake pipelines, signalling that fintech firms must urgently enhance verification protocols against increasingly realistic impersonation tactics.

last_week_digest.title: Home security giant ADT data breach affects 5.5 million people
last_week_digest.tldr: The ShinyHunters extortion group recently breached ADT, compromising the personal data of 5.5 million individuals after successfully executing a vishing attack against an employee’s single sign-on account. This incident, which exposed names, addresses, and partial government identifiers, highlights the severe operational risks posed by targeted social engineering against SaaS platforms. It signals a growing industry trend where attackers bypass robust perimeter defences by exploiting human-centric vulnerabilities within corporate ecosystems.

last_week_digest.title: Burglar alarm biz burgled: ADT confirms cyber intrusion after ShinyHunters extortion attempt
last_week_digest.tldr: Security firm ADT confirmed a cyber intrusion after the ShinyHunters gang claimed to have stolen over 10 million records from its cloud-based environments. While ADT downplayed the incident as a limited data breach, independent analysis suggests at least 5.5 million unique email addresses were compromised. This event signals a growing industry trend where attackers exploit SaaS platforms to bypass perimeter defences, severely damaging institutional trust and operational reputation.

last_week_digest.title: Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia-linked threat actor APT28 is actively exploiting an incomplete Windows patch to conduct zero-click attacks against organisations across Ukraine and the European Union. This security failure exposes critical operational infrastructure to remote compromise, highlighting the persistent risk of state-sponsored espionage. The incident signals a growing industry challenge where insufficient remediation of known vulnerabilities allows sophisticated adversaries to bypass existing defences, forcing businesses to re-evaluate their patch management lifecycles.

last_week_digest.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
last_week_digest.tldr: The North Korean group BlueNoroff is targeting cryptocurrency executives using AI-generated avatars and stolen video footage within fake Zoom meetings. By compromising at least 100 individuals, including numerous CEOs, the threat actors facilitate malware installation through deceptive software update prompts. This sophisticated campaign signals a shift toward weaponising trusted business communication tools, forcing organisations to reconsider the inherent security risks posed by routine virtual professional interactions globally.

last_week_digest.title: ShinyHunters exploit Anodot incident to target Vimeo
last_week_digest.tldr: Vimeo recently confirmed a security breach originating from third-party analytics provider Anodot, exposing metadata, video titles, and customer email addresses. The extortion group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility, threatening to leak stolen data from Snowflake and BigQuery environments unless a ransom is paid. This incident highlights the escalating systemic risk posed by third-party vendor vulnerabilities, signalling that supply chain security remains a critical, unresolved challenge for global digital enterprises.

last_week_digest.title: Signal Phishing Campaign Targets German Officials in Suspected Russian Operation
last_week_digest.tldr: Suspected Russian state-sponsored actors have launched a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting hundreds of German officials, including ministers, military personnel, and journalists, via the Signal messaging platform. By exploiting human trust rather than software vulnerabilities, attackers compromised private communications and sensitive political data. This operation signals a critical shift in global hybrid warfare, where threat actors bypass encryption by weaponising social engineering, highlighting the urgent need for enhanced human-centric security.

last_week_digest.title: China-linked hackers led phishing campaigns targeting journalists and activists, researchers say
last_week_digest.tldr: China-linked hackers utilised over 100 malicious domains to launch two sophisticated phishing campaigns, targeting journalists and activists across a nine-month period. By commissioning freelance contractors, state actors achieved low-cost digital repression while maintaining plausible deniability. This industrialized model signals a dangerous shift in global cyber threats, where offensive capabilities are increasingly commoditised, forcing organisations to recognise that human-centric social engineering remains a primary vector for compromising sensitive international networks.

last_week_digest.title: Critical GitHub Vulnerability Exposed Millions of Repositories
last_week_digest.tldr: A critical remote code execution vulnerability recently impacted GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server, potentially exposing millions of repositories to unauthorised access. This flaw poses significant operational risks, threatening the integrity of proprietary source code and intellectual property for global organisations. The incident signals a growing necessity for robust supply chain security, highlighting how centralised development platforms remain high-value targets that require rigorous, proactive patching to maintain enterprise trust.

last_week_digest.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
last_week_digest.tldr: The Vect 2.0 ransomware-as-a-service operation is inadvertently acting as a destructive wiper, permanently destroying files larger than 128KB across Windows, Linux, and VMware ESXi systems. Because the malware discards essential decryption nonces during encryption, victims cannot recover critical databases or backups even if they pay the ransom. This technical failure signals a growing trend of amateurish, flawed malware development that increases operational risk for global enterprises.

last_week_digest.title: CISA orders feds to patch Windows flaw exploited as zero-day
last_week_digest.tldr: CISA has mandated that federal agencies patch the CVE-2026-32202 Windows vulnerability by May 12 following active exploitation. This zero-click flaw, which facilitates NTLM hash theft and lateral network movement, stems from an incomplete previous security update. The incident highlights a growing industry trend where threat actors, including state-sponsored groups like APT28, exploit residual vulnerabilities to compromise sensitive data, forcing organisations to prioritise rapid remediation of recurring software weaknesses.

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft's patch for a 0-day exploited by Russian spies fell short. Another Windows flaw is under attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Microsoft has confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-32202, a zero-click Windows authentication coercion flaw resulting from an incomplete patch for a previous vulnerability linked to Russian state-sponsored actors. This security oversight allows attackers to harvest sensitive Net-NTLMv2 credentials, facilitating unauthorised network access and data theft. The incident highlights critical risks in patch efficacy, signalling that incomplete remediation efforts now represent a significant vector for sophisticated persistent threats globally.

last_week_digest.title: Large-scale Roblox hacking operation shut down by Ukrainian authorities
last_week_digest.tldr: Ukrainian authorities have dismantled a sophisticated criminal operation that compromised 610,000 Roblox accounts, generating approximately $225,000 in illicit profits through the sale of high-value digital assets. By exploiting session cookies, the suspects bypassed traditional security measures, highlighting significant vulnerabilities in user authentication protocols. This incident signals a growing trend where cybercriminals increasingly target virtual economies, forcing platforms to bolster account security to protect their global user base.

last_week_digest.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Checkmarx confirmed that attackers exfiltrated sensitive data from its GitHub environment on 30 March, following the injection of malicious code into its supply chain one week prior. This breach exposes the fragility of software development ecosystems, threatening downstream clients with potential supply chain compromises. It signals a critical industry shift where adversaries increasingly target trusted development platforms, forcing organisations to implement more rigorous integrity checks for third-party software.
